name: Unusual Chinese Grilled Shortribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the shortribs into short, flat strips. Place each rib fat-side-up on a cutting board and cut a 1/4-inch high layer almost (but not through) the bottom-most section of the rib. Turn the meat over and continue to make 1/4-inch layers until the rib is completely layered and now one long strip.
2. Pound the meat with the edge of a Chinese knife or other flat surface until it is evenly thickened. Chill the meat well; this cutting method does not work well on room temperature ribs.
3. MARINADE: Blend all liquid ingredients in a blender for about 15 seconds at "Blend" speed. Grate the fresh ginger roots and combine with minced garlic. Mince the white portion of the green onions. Add all ingredients to the blender mixture and operate on "low" for about 45 seconds.
4. Rub the sauce onto butterflied ribs and refrigerate for 2 or more hours.
5. Place ribs on the grill, basting often with sauce, until done (about 30 minutes depending on heat of grill). Watch carefully to determine when done. This sauce is excellent for chicken as well.
